description: Wasm baseline emitReturnCallRef mishandled multi-value stack results for return_call_ref tail calls. CVE-2024-9402 / MFSA 2024-50 cluster (sec-high, Firefox 130/131). In js/src/wasm/WasmBaselineCompile.cpp, BaseCompiler::emitReturnCallRef lowered a tail call through a function reference as if the callee produced a single result. For signatures with multiple stack-passed results, the baseline compiler failed to reserve and shuffle the full outgoing result area before the tail jump, leaving part of the caller frame footer uninitialized or overwritten. The next return, trap, or frame walk then read corrupted footer data and could jump through an attacker-shaped address. The fix makes emitReturnCallRef use the tail-call-aware result handling path, passing TailCallResults for the target signature into emitCallArgs so all result slots are laid out before the tail jump.
